title: Equipment Supply

What We Supply Train Safety Systems — SAUT, KLUB, BLOK, and other microprocessor-based systems Railway Components — sensors, controllers, communication modules, converters Industrial Equipment — measurement, diagnostic, and monitoring instruments Spare Parts — components for routine maintenance and repair Our Advantages Every shipment is accompanied by a complete documentation package: conformity certificates, quality passports, manufacturer declarations, and operation manuals. We handle customs clearance and logistics, providing a turnkey delivery experience.
To request a commercial quote, use our B2B platform or contact a manager directly.

title: Safety Systems for JSC Uzbekistan Railways
description: Supply and commissioning of microprocessor-based automatic braking control systems (SAUT) for the locomotive fleet.
content:
About the Project As part of the train safety system modernization program for JSC Uzbekistan Railways, RailTech supplied 42 sets of microprocessor-based automatic braking control systems (SAUT).
Objectives Supply certified equipment manufactured in Russia Full customs clearance and delivery to the client’s depot Technical support during commissioning Depot personnel training Results Equipment delivered on time and in full All sets successfully commissioned Personnel trained and certified 2-year warranty support provided This project was one of the largest in the company’s history, demonstrating our ability to execute large-scale government contracts.

Scope of Supply Portable rail track diagnostic instruments Switch and turnout monitoring systems Track geometry measurement devices Data analysis software Results The client received state-of-the-art equipment for conducting scheduled and unscheduled diagnostic inspections, improving both safety and efficiency of infrastructure maintenance.

title: Importing Industrial Equipment to Uzbekistan: A Practical Guide
description: A comprehensive guide to importing industrial equipment into Uzbekistan — customs procedures, certification, logistics, and common pitfalls.
content:
Importing industrial equipment into Uzbekistan is a process that demands attention to detail at every stage. In this guide, we share practical experience and recommendations.
Key Stages of Import 1. Documentation Preparation To import industrial equipment, you will need to prepare:
Contract with the foreign supplier Invoice and packing list Certificate of origin Technical documentation for the equipment 2. Customs Clearance Customs duties on industrial equipment in Uzbekistan range from 0% to 20% depending on the HS code. Preferential rates are available for certain categories of equipment not manufactured domestically.
3. Certification Most types of industrial equipment are subject to mandatory certification under the UzStandard system. The process includes:
Submitting an application to the certification body Document review Testing (if required) Issuance of a conformity certificate 4. Logistics Primary delivery routes for equipment to Uzbekistan:
Rail transport — optimal for heavy and oversized equipment Road transport — for urgent and smaller shipments Air freight — for mission-critical components Common Mistakes Incorrect product classification under the HS code Missing pre-import certification Improperly prepared contract documentation Ignoring labeling requirements How We Help RailTech manages the entire import process — from equipment selection to warehouse delivery, including customs clearance, certification, and logistics.

title: Train Safety in Uzbekistan: Current State and Future Outlook
description: An overview of train safety systems on the Uzbekistan Railways network — current infrastructure conditions, modernization plans, and the role of modern technology.
content:
Train safety is a top priority in the development of Uzbekistan’s railway sector. In this overview, we examine the current state of safety systems and the prospects for their modernization.
Current State Uzbekistan’s railway network spans over 6,500 km of track, serving both passenger and freight operations. The train safety system is built on several key components:
Automatic Block Signaling — interval-based train movement regulation Electric Interlocking — switch and signal control Automatic Locomotive Signaling — in-cab signal duplication Automatic Braking Systems — automated braking control Modernization Priorities The main modernization priorities include:
Transition to microprocessor-based control systems Implementation of real-time diagnostics and monitoring Upgrading lineside safety devices Digitalization of documentation and reporting workflows RailTech’s Role RailTech is actively contributing to the modernization process by supplying state-of-the-art equipment from leading global manufacturers and providing expert support throughout every stage of implementation.

title: RailTech Launches B2B Platform for Industrial Procurement
description: RailTech has unveiled a digital B2B platform that automates the full industrial equipment supply cycle — from catalog to closing documents.
content:
RailTech has launched its proprietary digital B2B platform, automating the full cycle of industrial equipment supply in Uzbekistan.
Platform Capabilities The platform integrates a catalog of 500+ equipment items, a quote calculator with Landed Cost computation, a CRM system for deal management, and a Partner Portal with KYC verification.
The standout feature is Smart Import, which allows users to upload an Excel file with their required items and automatically match them against the catalog. The algorithm achieves approximately 92% accuracy.
Who It’s For The platform is designed for industrial enterprises procuring equipment for:
Railway infrastructure Industrial manufacturing Safety and monitoring systems How to Get Started To access the platform, register in the Partner Portal and complete a quick company verification. Once verified, you will gain access to special pricing and the full suite of features.
Learn more about the platform’s capabilities on the Digital Platform page.
